title: Continuous function - Wikipedia
relevance: Offers a clear, formal epsilon‑delta definition of continuity and explains its relationship to limits.

relevance: Illustrates piecewise functions with removable holes and jump discontinuities, perfect for a visual Desmos activity.

heading: Formal Definition of Continuity
body: A function f is continuous at a point c if for every ε > 0 there exists a δ > 0 such that whenever |x − c| < δ, we have |f(x) − f(c)| < ε. This ε‑δ condition ensures that the values of f(x) can be made arbitrarily close to f(c) by taking x sufficiently close to c. In other words, the limit of f(x) as x approaches c equals f(c).

questions.q: Which of the following statements is equivalent to saying f is continuous at c?
questions.explanation: Continuity requires both the limit to exist and equal the function value at c.

questions.q: If a function has a removable discontinuity at x = 2, which of the following is true?
options: limₓ→2 f(x) exists but f(2) is undefined or different
Both left‑ and right‑hand limits are infinite
The function is not defined for x < 2
questions.explanation: A removable discontinuity means the limit exists, but the function’s value at that point is missing or mismatched.

questions.q: The Intermediate Value Theorem can be applied to which type of function on [a, b]?
options: A function that is differentiable on (a, b)
A function that is continuous on [a, b]
A function with a jump discontinuity at some point in (a, b)
questions.explanation: The IVT requires continuity on the closed interval; differentiability is not necessary, and jumps break the hypothesis.

questions.q: Suppose f is continuous on [0, 5] and f(0)=‑3, f(5)=7. Which value is guaranteed to be taken by f?
questions.explanation: By the IVT, every value between –3 and 7, including 0, must be attained somewhere in the interval.

title: Explore Piecewise Discontinuities
caption: Create a piecewise function that has a removable hole at x=1 and a jump at x=3. Observe left‑ and right‑hand limits and compare them to the function values.

title: Ask the Tutor
persona: You are a tutor for continuity and the Intermediate Value Theorem. Be concise and Socratic.
greeting: Hi! Ask me anything about why continuity matters for solving equations.
